prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: A Casserole for the Missing
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Court-Ordered Lifetime
Tagline: Every town has a basement.
Theme: Trust isn't given. It's rented.
Omen: a Christmas ornament turning up wet
Relationship dynamic: emotionally neglected marriage
Props: casserole dish, snow globe, blood-adjacent tea towel

Cast:
- Gavin Pike | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=bakery owner | threat_aura=0.34 | space=unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files | secrets=once left town without explaining why; has an unsent email that would destroy a family
- Evelyn Bennett | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=hardware store owner | threat_aura=0.47 | space=toolshed described as retreat supplies | secrets=knows where evidence should have been
- Noah Bennett | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=real estate agent | threat_aura=0.55 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=may die first or survive unbelievably
- Rachel Winterson | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=therapist | threat_aura=0.56 | space=old storm shelter from before the divorce | secrets=had brunch with someone officially deceased; knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
- Derek Lark | archetype=soft_spoken_menace | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=sommelier with unusual patience | threat_aura=0.62 | space=storage unit described as donation overflow | secrets=rehearsed his alibi before anyone asked; knows exactly how long the sheriff takes to respond
- Derek Mercer | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=substitute teacher | threat_aura=0.31 | space=old family office described as retreat supplies | secrets=saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it
- Claire Vale | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Daniel Bell | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Evelyn Bennett is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Gavin Pike says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Bellweather.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Evelyn?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Claire. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Gavin. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The casserole dish is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Rachel."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
